content:
...with a nod or a tray of buns. But the cellar door is open now. 

I look at Levi, really seeing the way he stands there, caught between the street and our table. I see the hesitation in the set of his shoulders, a mirror of the man I used to be just a few months ago—always looking for the quickest way back into the shadows. I reach out, my hand steady, and tap the surface of the table twice. The
